title: Spain XI vs Austria: Starting lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today
original_text:
Spain XI vs Austria: Starting lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today
Spain XI vs Saudi Arabia: Lamine Yamal IN - starting lineup, confirmed team news for World Cup today
Lamine Yamal has admitted that he is not yet ready to play a full 90 minutes as Spain prepare to face Saudi Arabia in the second of their World Cup 2026 group stage games, but will start against Saudi Arabia regardless.
The Barcelona starlet came into this tournament with a hamstring problem that saw him miss the end of the season, but he returned to action in Spain’s shock 0-0 draw with Cape Verde in their opener.
Advertisement
FOLLOW SPAIN VS SAUDI ARABIA LIVE!
He came on the right flank for Luis de la Fuente’s side, but was given just 20 minutes to make an impact as the European champions were frustrated by the minnows.
Now, the Barcelona star has issued an update on his fitness ahead of taking on the Saudis in Atlanta, Georgia.
"I'm fine, I'm feeling good, but it's too soon, it's unnecessary,” Yamal told TVE about playing a full game.
“I'm still settling in – it's not the right time to play a full match yet – but I can play for as many minutes as the manager wants.”
Advertisement
Lamine Yamal is not fully fit after a hamstring issue (Getty)
Gavi was the starting left winger but was replaced by Nico Williams against Cape Verde, while Rodri, Pedri and Fabian Ruiz were the midfield three.
Don’t expect the engine room to change, but if Williams is fully fit, he is likely to come in on the left, despite an injury-hit campaign at Athletic Club.
In defence, Marcos Llorente, a natural midfielder, was at right-back but is replaced by Pedro Porro here , while Marc Cucurella, now of Real Madrid, is on the left.
Advertisement
Pau Cubarsi and Aymeric Laporte will continue in the middle.
There is a blow for De La Fuente, with Victor Munoz, now of Liverpool, suffering a setback in his recovery from an underlying calf problem that he arrived in north America with.
The Spanish FA released a statement on the winger, saying: "During his scheduled and individualised recovery process, an additional muscle injury has occurred, which will delay his return to competition," the RFEF said about Munoz's latest injury.
Advertisement
"His availability for upcoming matches will depend on his progress and rehabilitation in the coming days."
Spain XI (4-3-3): Simon; Porro, Cubarsi, Laporte, Cucurella; Rodri, Olmo, Pedri; Yamal, Baena, Oyarzabal
Injured: Munoz
Date and time: 5pm BST on Sunday, June 21, 2026
Venue: Atlanta Stadium, Georgia
How to watch: BBC
_note: 全文走 summary 块A(LLM三判后只增不改);processed=成熟仓库累积,raw=本次收集过程。
